title: centaurea ispahanica subsp. sirjanensis
intro: Centaurea ispahanica subsp. sirjanensis, also known as Sirjan knapweed, is a plant species in the daisy family Asteraceae, native to Iran. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Centaurea ispahanica subsp. sirjanensis include its potential to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory problems, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate pain. In herbal preparations, the plant's flowers and leaves are often dried and used to make teas, infusions, and topical applications, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
